content.question: What share of current demand for 6/8-year gimnázium tracks is motivated by genuine academic differentiation versus parent aspirations for university entry signaling, and how would demand respond to counterfactual expansion of alternative selective mechanisms (e.g., specialized upper-secondary vocational tracks, external examination pathways)?
content.reduction_path: Design survey of parent/student cohorts in selective-track schools (stratified by Budapest vs. provincial, public vs. church vs. elite state) with discrete-choice scenarios; examine application data for alternative routes (e.g., specialized vocational, external exam access) as revealed-preference benchmark.
